row1.revenue: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with $716.9B in revenue, narrowly surpassing Walmart Inc. (WMT) by $3.7B. The top two companies hold a significant revenue lead over the next closest competitor, UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (UNH), with a $269.4B gap.
row1.net_income: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a Net Income of $132.2B, a significant gap of $30.1B over NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) in second place. This substantial lead highlights GOOGL's dominance in the tech sector.
row1.total_assets: JPMorgan Chase leads with $4.4T in Total Assets, a significant gap from the second-largest bank, BAC, with $3.4T, highlighting JPM's substantial market presence.
row1.total_equity: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads with $415.3B in Total Equity, narrowly surpassing Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) by $4.2B. The top 2 companies hold 54% of the total equity among the 5 listed.
row1.total_liabilities: JPM leads the pack with $4.1T in Total Liabilities, a significant gap from second-place BAC at $3.1T, highlighting the substantial debt burden of major financial institutions.
row1.cash_and_equivalents: JPMorgan Chase (JPM) holds the top spot with $343.3B in Cash And Equivalents, a significant lead over the next closest competitor, Bank of America (BAC) with $239.3B. This gap of over $104B underscores JPM's substantial liquidity advantage.
row1.gross_profit: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a Gross Profit of 360.5B USD, nearly 50% more than the second-place Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) at 240.3B USD. This significant gap underscores Amazon's dominance in the Gross Profit category.
row1.operating_income_ebit: Apple Inc. (AAPL) leads the pack with a staggering Operating Income Ebit of $133.1B USD, outpacing NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) by $2.7B USD. The top 2 companies hold a significant $49.7B USD gap in Operating Income Ebit over the 3rd ranked GOOGL.

row2_sector.revenue: Consumer Cyclical and Technology sectors dominate the revenue landscape, accounting for 43.1% of total revenue with 976.4B USD and 937.6B USD respectively. This concentration highlights the significant influence these sectors have on overall market performance.
row2_sector.total_assets: Financial Services sector dominates the industry with 64.1% of total assets, a significant concentration of wealth and influence. This dominance is nearly 3 times the combined share of the next three largest sectors: Technology, Communication Services, and Consumer Cyclical.
row2_sector.net_income: Technology sector dominates the net income landscape with 39.2% share, a significant 14.4 percentage point lead over the second-largest sector. Communication Services is the only other sector with a double-digit percentage share.

row2_bubble: NVDA stands out with a 55.6% net margin, significantly outperforming UNH's 2.7% margin, highlighting the substantial difference in profitability efficiency between these two companies. This gap underscores NVDA's ability to maintain high profit margins despite its large scale, as indicated by its 215.9B USD revenue.
row3_heatmap: Morgan Stanley (MA) stands out for its exceptional return on equity (193.5%), while JPMorgan Chase (JPM) is the most leveraged with a debt-to-equity ratio of 11.21x, indicating a higher risk profile.
row4_market_trends: Alphabet (GOOGL) leads the pack with a 12-month cumulative return of +89.4%, outpacing the market significantly. Conversely, Netflix (NFLX) has struggled, posting a -45.9% return over the same period. This stark contrast highlights the divergent fortunes of high-revenue companies in recent market trends.
